model.label: Grok 4.2
model.description: xAI • Large reasoning model designed for real-time information access, coding, and general problem solving.

pick.rationale: Arkansas holds a slight edge in both KenPom (17th) and Torvik (20th) over Wisconsin (22nd and 21st), with a markedly superior adjusted offense ranking top-5 nationally from a demanding SEC schedule that prepares them well for March. The Razorbacks' 25-8 record and efficiency on both ends create a modest but consistent advantage in this neutral-site Round of 32 game against Wisconsin's solid but slightly lower-ranked Big Ten metrics. While Wisconsin's shooting big men offer a credible upset path that has drawn praise from analysts like Jay Bilas, the overall quality gap and lack of major reported injuries tilt toward Arkansas advancing.

reasoningStep.title: Ratings, injuries, and expert context
reasoningStep.summary: Advanced metrics show Arkansas with a narrow but clear advantage in offense and overall rank; no major injuries noted for either side in recent searches or prior picks; Bilas highlights Wisconsin's potential but metrics support the higher-ranked Razorbacks.
evidence: KenPom/Torvik data: Arkansas 17/20 (adjO top-5), Wisconsin 22/21; records 25-8 vs 24-10
Web searches confirm close matchup with Wisconsin bigs as key but no health red flags altering the projection
Prior bracket picks show both teams advanced comfortably without noted concerns

result.title: No. 2 Arizona earns top seed in West Region of NCAA Tournament | AP News
result.snippet: The West also includes No. 2 seed Purdue, Gonzaga, Arkansas and Wisconsin in a region that goes through San Jose, California, on the way to the Final Four in Indianapolis. The Wildcats haven't been to the Final Four since 2001, but have the type of team that could end that streak.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
Bracket config id: demo-2026-bracket
Tournament year: 2026
Current game id: r32-west-2
Current game label: West Round of 32 2
Round: Round of 32
Resolved teams: Wisconsin (wisconsin) vs Arkansas (arkansas)
Prior committed picks: 41
